subclassFeature.name: Chronal Shift
entries: At tier 1, you can magically exert limited control over the flow of time around a creature. As a reaction, after you or a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a save, you can force the creature to reroll. You make this decision after the DM conveys the results of the roll. The trget must use the result of the second roll.
You can use this feature twice, and you regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Temporal Awareness
entries: Starting at tier 1, you can add your Intelligence modifier to your initiative rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Chronurgy Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Chronurgy Wizards have access to the following major effect options:

items: The initiative order is immediately revealed to you. Then, you may move an ally on the initiative tracker to any position on the tracker.

You warp time around the target, causing them to become {@condition rattled} and suffer {@dice 1d8} ongoing force harm, stacking until they pass a passive save. This harm increases to {@dice 2d8} at 5th level, {@dice 3d8} at 11th level, and {@dice 4d8} at 17th level.

subclassFeature.name: Momentary Stasis
entries: When you reach tier 3, as an action, you can magically make a fortitude attack against a Large or smaller creature within 60 feet of you, with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is encased in a field of magical energy until the end of your next turn or until it takes any damage. While encased in this way, the creature is {@condition slowed}.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, but the creature is {@condition unconscious} while encased instead of {@condition slowed}.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the target is also {@condition slowed} for 1 round after it is released from the field.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Altered Fate
entries: From tier 3, after you influence an attack with your Chronal Shift feature, you may choose to also either increase or decrease the attack's success tier by one.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Abeyance
entries: At tier 4, when you cast a spell using a spell slot of 4th level or lower, you can condense the spell's magic into a mote. The spell is frozen in time at the moment of casting and held within a grey bead for 1 hour. This bead is a Tiny object with 2 armour and 2 hit points, and it is immune to poison and psychic damage and harm. When the duration ends, or if the bead is destroyed, it vanishes in a flash of light, and the spell is lost.

A creature holding the bead can use its action to release the spell within, whereupon the bead disappears. The spell uses your spellcasting ability, but otherwise treats the creature who released it as the caster for all other purposes.
Once you create a bead with this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Convergent Future
entries: Starting at tier 5, you can peer through possible futures and magically pull one of them into events around you, ensuring a particular outcome. When you or a creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a save, you can use your reaction to ignore the die roll and immediately decide whether it succeeds or fails (or, in the case of an attack, whether it scores a standard or minor success).
When you use this feature, you gain one level of {@condition strife}. Strife gained in this way are lost whenever you take a long rest, but cannot be removed in any other way.
